concerns.analysis: The source document contains an agency logo for DEQ (Department of Environmental Quality) at the bottom left of the first page, as well as the signature of David Miller in the sign-off block. Neither of these meaningful images has a corresponding tag in the HTML output. While an image corresponding to the State Seal at the top of page 1 is present, the distinct DEQ logo and the signature are completely omitted.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: DEQ logo at the bottom left of page 1, and David Miller's signature above his printed name.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains text for the footer ('North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality...') and the sign-off ('Sincerely, David Miller, PE...') but no elements are present for the logo or signature.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (2 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=2. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Scanned document: source has 0 extractable text but output contains 377 words (OCR'd from images)
concerns.analysis: baseline_words=0, gemini_words=377. Entire document is image-based; output likely duplicates content as both page image and extracted text.

concerns.description: Output has more images than source PDF (ratio 2.5x) — possible image duplication
concerns.analysis: image_count_ratio=2.50. More images in output than in source indicates duplicated or fabricated image elements.
The source document uses an underline to emphasize the phrase 'within sixty (60) days' on the second page. This underline is lost in the extracted output.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (1): North Carolina Department of Environmental Quality | Divisio
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=1. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.
